task_completion.description: Import the three video clips into the Video Sequence Editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender Video Sequence Editor timeline shows at least three distinct movie strips (clip_A, clip_B, clip_C), which may appear as blue, amber, and green strips.

task_completion.description: Add a cross-dissolve or gamma cross transition between adjacent clips.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A transition strip (e.g., 'Gamma Cross' or 'Cross') is visible in the VSE timeline overlapping or placed between the movie strips.

task_completion.description: Add a text strip titled 'BMW Showcase Reel'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text strip is visible in the VSE timeline, and the text 'BMW Showcase Reel' is visible in the video preview area or the text strip properties panel.

task_completion.description: Set resolution to 960x540, framerate to 24fps, and adjust the scene frame range.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Output Properties panel, Resolution X is 960, Y is 540, Frame Rate is 24 fps, and the Frame Start/End values are adjusted to cover the full length of the sequence.

task_completion.description: Set the render output to FFmpeg video (H.264/MPEG-4) and specify the output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Output Properties panel, the Output path is set to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/final_edit.mp4', File Format is 'FFmpeg Video', and the encoding container/codec is set to MPEG-4/H.264.

task_completion.description: Render the final animation and save the Blender project file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender 'Render Animation' window is shown processing frames, and/or a terminal/file manager shows 'final_edit.mp4' and 'video_edit.blend' successfully created in '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender GUI to perform the video editing tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Blender interface with the Video Sequence Editor open and actively used, rather than just executing a pre-written Python script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The agent actually rendered the video using Blender.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender render window or rendering progress bar is visible in the screenshots, indicating the software is actually processing the video frames.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to create fake empty output files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal does not show commands like 'touch /home/ga/BlenderProjects/final_edit.mp4' or 'touch /home/ga/BlenderProjects/video_edit.blend'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires assembling 3 video clips (clip_A.mp4, clip_B.mp4, clip_C.mp4) in Blender's Video Sequence Editor. The clips are 2 seconds each and have distinct colored backgrounds (blue, amber, green). The sequence must contain at least 3 movie strips, at least 1 transition effect (cross-dissolve/gamma cross), and a text strip containing the words 'bmw' and 'showcase' (case-insensitive). The final project must be saved to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/video_edit.blend' and the rendered video to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/final_edit.mp4'.
